decks.notes: The main concept of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan through a combination of efficient counterspells, discard spells, and removal. The deck aims to slow down the opponent's progression by denying their key spells and creatures. It also utilizes powerful finishers like Murktide Regent and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares to close out the game once the opponent's resources have been depleted. Additionally, the deck includes card advantage engines like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Cling to Dust to maintain card advantage and control the game in the long run.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. By utilizing cards like Counterspell, Fatal Push, and Drown in the Loch, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while protecting its own win conditions. Additionally, cards like Snapcaster Mage and Sheoldred's Edict provide additional value by recurring spells or removing threats from the opponent's side of the battlefield. The deck also includes card draw spells like Consult the Star Charts and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian to ensure a steady stream of resources.

The main idea of this deck is to utilize instant reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance to quickly bring back powerful creatures like Griselbrand or Atraxa, Grand Unifier from the graveyard and combo off for a win. Cards like Psychic Frog and Fallaji Archaeologist help with card advantage and filtering, while disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation help protect the combo. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and instant speed plays.

This deck focuses on controlling the board through counterspells, removal, and card advantage provided by cards like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Teferi, Time Raveler. Isochron Scepter helps to lock down opponents with spells like Orim's Chant and Prismatic Ending. Solitude offers a powerful removal option while Kaheera, the Orphanguard provides additional value as a companion. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and outvalue them over time with efficient answers and card advantage spells.

The deck focuses on utilizing Broodscale Bloodchief as a central combo piece, supported by cards like Blade of the Bloodchief, Malevolent Rumble, and Glaring Fleshraker to generate powerful synergies and overwhelm opponents. It also includes key elements like Urza's Saga for additional utility and Ancient Stirrings for consistency in finding key pieces. The deck aims to control the board with disruptive spells like Unholy Heat and Kozilek's Command while setting up for a game-ending combo with Emrakul, the Promised End. Flexibility is offered through cards like Soul-Guide Lantern and Vexing Bauble for varied strategies.

The deck focuses on utilizing fast mana acceleration spells like Desperate Ritual and Pyretic Ritual to cast multiple spells in a single turn, building up to a big combo turn with Ral, Monsoon Mage and Ruby Medallion to generate a large amount of mana and play multiple spells. The deck also includes cards like Past in Flames and Wish to recur key spells from the graveyard and search for specific combo pieces. The goal is to chain together spells, generate a massive amount of mana, and overwhelm the opponent with powerful sorceries and instants.

This deck revolves around quickly generating mana with cards like Pyretic Ritual and Ruby Medallion to cast powerful spells like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Past in Flames. The goal is to create a storm of spell casting, chaining together multiple spells in a turn to overwhelm the opponent and finish them off with a massive Grapeshot. The deck also utilizes cards like Wrenn's Resolve and Hex Magic to protect its combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan, while using Wish to search for key cards when needed.

This deck focuses on assembling the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Tower, Mine, and Power Plant) to generate a large amount of mana quickly. It uses powerful Eldrazi creatures and control cards to disrupt the opponent's game plan and overpower them with big threats like Karn, the Great Creator and Emrakul, the Promised End. The deck also includes various spells and artifacts to control the board and prevent the opponent from executing their strategy effectively. It aims to dominate the late game with its high-powered creatures and powerful spells.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy, utilizing efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Territorial Kavu, backed up by Leyline of the Guildpact and Leyline Binding to control the board. Quantum Riddler and Scion of Draco provide additional threats while cards like Lightning Bolt and Stubborn Denial offer removal and protection. The deck also includes some versatility with cards like Consign to Memory and Teferi, Time Raveler, allowing for different lines of play depending on the situation. Overall, the deck aims to quickly build a strong board presence and maintain pressure throughout the game.

The core strategy of this deck is to quickly reanimate powerful creatures such as Ulamog, the Defiler and Griselbrand using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Consign to Memory. Atraxa, Grand Unifier helps to protect your combo pieces and disrupt your opponent's game plan. Thoughtseize and Force of Negation provide additional disruption to control the game while Faithless Looting helps filter through your deck to find your combo pieces. Emperor of Bones serves as a backup win condition in case your primary reanimation targets are countered or removed. Overall, the deck focuses on fast, explosive plays to overwhelm your opponent.

The focus of this Grixis Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan through a combination of hand disruption, counterspells, removal, and card advantage. By utilizing cards like Thoughtseize, Counterspell, Fatal Push, and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, the deck aims to dismantle the opponent's strategy while generating advantage through card draw and tempo plays. With a mix of control elements and threats like Snapcaster Mage and Psychic Frog, this deck seeks to outlast the opponent and grind out a victory through careful resource management and strategic decision-making.

This deck aims to quickly ramp up mana using Eldrazi Temple and Urza's Saga to play big threats like Emrakul, the Promised End. It utilizes Writhing Chrysalis and Basking Broodscale to create tokens and buffs them with Blade of the Bloodchief. Malevolent Rumble can clear the board for a final swing. Boseiju, Who Endures helps protect important spells like Kozilek's Command. Vexing Bauble provides card advantage and control. Overall, the deck focuses on creating a powerful board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on quick and aggressive creature attacks, utilizing cards such as Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to deal damage swiftly. It also includes cards like Goblin Bombardment for additional direct damage, and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah for board control. The deck has a low mana curve and aims to overwhelm the opponent early in the game, potentially closing out the match before the opponent can fully set up their own strategy. Overall, the deck prioritizes speed and aggression to secure victory.

The main concept of this Boros Ponza control deck is to disrupt the opponent's mana base and resources with land destruction spells like Cleansing Wildfire and Field of Ruin, while using removal spells like Path to Exile and Wrath of God to control the board. The deck also aims to generate value through cards like Avengers Disassembled and Price of Freedom, and utilize creatures like Solitude and Galvanic Discharge for additional control and threats. Additionally, the deck includes graveyard hate with cards like Rest in Peace to disrupt opponents relying on graveyard strategies.

This deck focuses on assembling the UrzaTron lands combo to generate large amounts of colorless mana quickly. It also utilizes Eldrazi creatures and powerful control spells to disrupt the opponent's game plan and apply pressure. The goal is to ramp up mana, control the board with powerful Eldrazi creatures and spells, and ultimately overwhelm the opponent with big threats like Emrakul, the Promised End and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms. The deck aims to control the game and win through powerful late-game plays.

The main concept of this deck is to aggressively control the board and lower your own life total to maximize the power of Death's Shadow. Using cards like Mishra's Bauble and Street Wraith to draw cards and manipulate your life total, along with disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Stubborn Denial to disrupt your opponent's strategy. Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Quantum Riddler provide additional threats and card advantage. The deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and efficient removal spells in order to secure a victory.
